frameworks.name: binding-by-synchrony
frameworks.one_line: phase-synchronous firing binds semantic features with working-memory concepts

frameworks.name: phase comparison mechanism
frameworks.one_line: phase relations between assemblies subserve response selection in comparison tasks

core_question: How do perceptual inputs, working memory, and semantic memory interact during semantic comparison of sequential stimuli?
key_assumptions: neural assemblies encode features and concepts via synchronous firing
phase relations carry task-relevant comparison information
semantic processing requires binding of features to working-memory concepts
manipulations: sequential presentation of two stimuli requiring semantic feature comparison
key_findings: simulated model reproduces semantic differentiation of sequential stimuli
phase-synchronous firing binds semantic features with working-memory concepts
phase comparison mechanism supports response selection
interpretations: binding-by-synchrony accounts extend to semantic cognition with WM interaction
task-dependent gamma-band synchronization between temporal and prefrontal cortex is predicted during semantic comparison
